metadata.procedure_phase: Stage 3 CLOSED at commit c894ebd (Stage 3.10 closure VERIFIED via three-subagent convergence; ack g7-ea7_n14 sub-clauses a-d binding 5 new findings dispositions). EA-7 has retired correctly. Stage 4 (Cutover per docs/platform_separation/05_STAGE4_CUTOVER.xml) NOT YET SPAWNED. Guide-#8's first substantive task is the Variant B comprehension gate; second task is to consider a Stage 4.0.v2 pre-flight audit checkpoint (per Guide-#7 methodology; see decision d10 below); third task is to author the Stage 4 EA-8 initialization pointer per the established pattern.
metadata.branch_at_handoff_blarai: chore/platform-extraction @ c894ebd (Stage 3.10 close VERIFIED; 5 docs-only commits ahead of Stage 2 close 3d1ac35: 77e26a9 → 97effe2 → 3118178 → 865c52f → 76f6050 → c894ebd)
metadata.branch_at_handoff_devplatform: main @ 95884a0 (Stage 3 single substantive commit; 189 files / +39592 -2; parent 3894221) — IMMUTABLE through Stage 4 entry
metadata.status_md_last_commit: 76f6050 (Stage 3 STATUS append: +59/-5; 6-item Stage 3 Closure Footnotes section added; Anomalies A12 + A13 + A14 added; Stage Ledger Stage 3 row PENDING → COMPLETE; Current State table updated for Stage 3 close + Stage 4 transition)

fleet_pause_state.verified_by_predecessor: confirmed at every disposition cycle + at Stage 3.10 close via S2 subagent

metadata.fleet_pause_state: paused with original reason "platform separation v2 in progress" + last_updated_by="copilot_agent" + last_updated_utc="2026-04-24T20:46:12Z" — preserved verbatim through 4-day Stage 2 → Stage 3 procedure cycle. NEVER state.resume_fleet was called across the entire Stage 2 + Stage 3 procedure. state.json sha256 = `71fc1a2f21177f2461a750d0239244e709cf1e228778c46a6e188cc50d2d5d19` (BYTE-IDENTICAL pre-Stage-2 → Stage-3-close).
metadata.worktree_state_blarai: clean (zero entries; git status --porcelain returns empty).
metadata.worktree_state_devplatform: NOT clean: ` M .gitignore` with 7-line tail-block duplication (functionally inert; gitignore tolerates duplicates). EA-7's "Working tree: clean" claim was FALSE at audit time. Bound as NEW rr8 forward-carry per g7-ea7_n14(a): Stage 4 EA-8 SHALL execute `git checkout -- .gitignore` on devplatform AS THE FIRST PREFLIGHT ACTION.
metadata.pytest_baseline: BlarAI side (pytest tools/): 351 passed (held throughout Stage 3; runtime-no-op for STATUS-only mutation). devplatform side (pytest --collect-only tools/): 351 tests collected, 0 errors (per Stage 3 EA-7 §4(b) discharge of stop-condition (ii); same count as BlarAI per byte-identical chokepoint copies).
metadata.stash_zero: stash@{0}: On main: wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260424_013002 — byte-identical timestamp held since v3 emission; 13+-cycle preservation through Stage 2 (v3-2.10.1) + Stage 3 (init pointer + iter-1 + 3.1.v2 + 3.2.v2 + 3.0.v2 audit checkpoint + Stage 3 close + 3.10 close verified). NEVER touched. The cleanest single-axis discipline win in the procedure.
metadata.trigger_reason: la_request "approve handoff" + stage_boundary_recommendation per GUIDE_PROMPT.xml <trigger name="stage_boundary_recommendation"> (HIGH-risk Stage 4 imminent; "successor benefits from a clean slate at high-risk boundaries"). Guide-#7 proactively recommended handoff at Stage 3 close (per LA chat agreement earlier in session); LA approved with "author the handoff xml" signal. Option B (handoff to Guide-#8) selected over Option A (continue with Guide-#7).

guide_handoff_brief.active_conversational_topic:
Stage 3 has just CLOSED at all 4 axes (structural + narrative + audit-chain + invariant) per the Guide-#7 closure verdict (docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/3.10-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_7_STAGE3_CLOSE_VERIFIED.xml) supported by triple-subagent verification evidence at 3.10-AUDIT_SUBAGENTS_S1_S2_S3_STAGE3_CLOSE_VERIFICATION_EVIDENCE.md. EA-7 has retired correctly. Stage 4 (Cutover per docs/platform_separation/05_STAGE4_CUTOVER.xml — HIGH risk, single_session, continuous_block) NOT YET SPAWNED.
The LA's IMMEDIATE next steps per Option B selection: (1) LA spawns fresh Guide-#8 chat with: GUIDE_PROMPT.xml + STATUS.md + GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml + Variant B chat-init block. (2) Guide-#8 runs Variant B comprehension gate (re-reads all 11-item source-doc set + 16 Guide-#6 dispositions + 9 Guide-#5 dispositions + 5 EA mid-stage handoffs + this handoff brief + 4 Guide-#7 verdict files at temp_for_responses/3.X — 3.0.v2 + 3.1.v2 + 3.2.v2 + 3.10 + audit evidence files). (3) Guide-#8 considers Stage 4.0.v2 pre-flight audit checkpoint (per Guide-#7 procedural-inheritance methodology; see decision d10). RECOMMENDED but NOT mandatory. (4) Guide-#8 authors the Stage 4 EA-8 initialization pointer per the established pattern. (5) LA spawns Stage 4 EA-8 with the init pointer + 05_STAGE4_CUTOVER.xml + standard attachment set.
Guide-#8's IMMEDIATE substantive tasks (in order, after Variant B gate): (a) Acknowledge the Stage 3 close + flag any drift since 2026-04-28T01:30:00Z (compare branch_at_handoff_blarai c894ebd, branch_at_handoff_devplatform 95884a0, status_md_last_commit 76f6050, worktree state per metadata against actual repo state). (b) Decide on Stage 4.0.v2 audit checkpoint (recommended per d10). (c) If audit checkpoint chosen: author the Stage 4.0.v2 audit subagent prompt; spawn it; synthesize findings into a Stage 4.0.v2 verdict (binding any spec amendments via cumulative-ack inheritance under new ack g8-ea8_n1). (d) Author the Stage 4 EA-8 initialization pointer.

decision.title: Stage 3 closure structure: 5 Guide-#7 verdict files + 1 Stage 3 EA close commit + 1 Guide-#7 closure verdict commit
decision.what: Stage 3 closed via 6 commits in sequence: - 77e26a9 (Stage 3 init pointer + EA-7 iter-1 gate + Guide-#7 iter-1 verdict APPROVED) - 97effe2 (Guide-#7 Item 3.2.v2 verdict — selective governance copy + Stage 3.3 forward-bind + Stage 4/5 two-layer audit directive; ack g7-ea1_n12) - 3118178 (Orphaned 3.1.v2 verdict cleanup commit — escalation_watchdog filename correction; ack g7-ea1_n11) - 865c52f (Guide-#7 Stage 3.X comprehensive audit checkpoint at Item 3.0.v2 — drives EA-7's Item 3.4 STOP disposition + binds Items 3.4-3.10.v2 amendments under ack g7-ea1_n13 with 8 sub-clauses (a)-(h); audit subagent evidence MD committed alongside) - 76f6050 (EA-7 Stage 3 close — STATUS.md +59/-5 only; sole-mutation invariant honored; 6-item Stage 3 Closure Footnotes section + A12/A13/A14 anomalies) - c894ebd (Guide-#7 Stage 3.10 closure VERIFIED via triple-subagent convergence — S1 devplatform technical, S2 BlarAI sole-mutation, S3 procedural completeness; ack g7-ea7_n14 sub-clauses a-d binding 5 new findings dispositions; audit evidence MD committed alongside) Total ack chain depth at Stage 3 close: 55 v4-era + 4 g6-ea5 + 10 g6-ea6 + 5 g7-ea1_n10..n13 + 1 g7-ea7_n14 = 75 acks all SATISFIED at appropriate Item closures.
decision.successor_instruction: Guide-#8 inherits this 6-commit chain. The single Stage-3 substantive commit is on devplatform side (95884a0 — 189 files / +39592 -2 / parent 3894221) NOT BlarAI side; Stage 3 BlarAI side was strictly STATUS-append-only (sole-mutation invariant). Guide-#8 should familiarize with the 4 Guide-#7 verdict files at temp_for_responses/ for full Stage 3 disposition history.

decision.title: EA-numbering convention correction: GLOBAL not stage-scoped
decision.what:
At 2026-04-27 LA explicitly corrected: Stage 3 EA is EA-7 (not EA-1). Global EA-numbering convention continues across stages: Stage 1 = EA-1; Stage 2 = EA-2..EA-6 (5 cycles); Stage 3 = EA-7. Each new EA continues the count regardless of stage boundary. Ack-naming convention follows: `g{guide_instance}-ea{ea_global_num}_n{seq}`. Stage 2 precedent (g6-ea6_n7..n9) confirms global convention.
DEFECT: Guide-#7 verdict files at temp_for_responses/ (3.0.v2, 3.1.v2, 3.2.v2 + the 3.0 init pointer authored before LA correction) use stage-scoped `g7-ea1_n*` naming. Per no-amend doctrine for committed audit artifacts, those labels are NOT retroactively rewritten; correction folds into Stage 6.7.5 TICKET A scope (see d8 below).
Guide-#7's 3.10 closure verdict (committed at c894ebd) is the FIRST verdict to use the corrected `g7-ea7_n14` naming.
decision.successor_instruction:
Guide-#8 SHALL emit all new acks under `g8-ea8_n*` per global convention. Cumulative-ack chain at Stage 3 close: - g7-ea1_n10 (Iter 1 verdict; verbatim per no-amend) - g7-ea1_n11 (3.1.v2 escalation_watchdog filename; verbatim) - g7-ea1_n12 (3.2.v2 selective governance copy + 4 hardening tickets; verbatim) - g7-ea1_n13 (3.0.v2 audit checkpoint, 8 sub-clauses a-h; verbatim) - g7-ea7_n14 (3.10 closure VERIFIED, 4 sub-clauses a-d; corrected naming)
EA-8 must restate this entire chain verbatim (including the documentation-defect labels for n10..n13) at iter-1 comprehension gate. Anomaly A12 records the no-amend disposition; future stages CONTINUE to restate verbatim.

decision.title: Item 3.1.v2 spec correction: escalation_watchdog filename hyphen → underscore
decision.what: Stage 3 v2_updates Item 3.1.v2 referenced `escalation-watchdog.ps1` (hyphen). Actual filename is `escalation_watchdog.ps1` (underscore). Spec-correction option (a) authorized. Bound at ack g7-ea1_n11.
decision.successor_instruction: Guide-#8 inherits the corrected filename baseline. Stage 5 EA's removal pattern (and any Stage 4 reference to the file) MUST use the underscore form. Stage 6.7.5 TICKET A scope absorbs the locked Stage 3 XML correction.

decision.title: Item 3.2.v2 selective governance copy + F-N1..F-N11 noted-for-Stage-EA findings
decision.what: Stage 3 spec said copy entire `docs/governance/` to devplatform. Actual classification: 4 of 17 governance files are fleet-shared (3 from spec + STYLE.md added per X-COMPREHENSIVE) → COPY to devplatform; remaining 13 BlarAI-runtime SOPs preserve in BlarAI, do NOT copy. Plus 11 noted-for-Stage-EA findings (F-N1..F-N11) about additional sweep items surfaced in passing. Disposition: option X-COMPREHENSIVE 7-step plan authorized; ack g7-ea1_n12 bound; 4 Stage 6.7.5 hardening tickets (A through D) created.
decision.successor_instruction: Stage 4 EA-8 does NOT re-investigate F-N1..F-N11. F-N5/F-N6/F-N7 path drifts forward-bound to Stage 5 EA fresh-eyes (per f5_1). F-N8 BlarAI/.vscode/tasks.json post-Stage-5 cleanup forward-bound to Stage 5 EA (per f5_2; n13(c)(iii) closes the dependency). F-N10 Stage 5.6 testpaths-removal pattern miss for `tools/gate_stale_cleaner/tests` forward-bound to Stage 5 EA (per f5_3, HIGH priority). F-N11 promotion (Class D → C) discharged at n13(f) Item 3.4.5 NEW.

decision.title: Item 3.0.v2 Stage 3.X comprehensive audit checkpoint (procedural innovation)
decision.what: EA-7 hit THREE compound spec drifts in succession (Items 3.1.v2 + 3.2.v2 + 3.4 at the BlarAI/pyproject.toml [project]-block-missing trap). Reactive STOP-cycle pattern (one verdict per drift) was non-converging. LA approved a procedural restructuring: insert a Stage 3.X comprehensive audit checkpoint BEFORE EA-7 resumes Item 3.4. Fresh general-purpose subagent spawned by Guide-#7; ~48 min runtime; 170,332 tokens; 114 tool uses; produced drift inventory across remaining Items 3.4-3.10.v2. Findings consolidated under one ack g7-ea1_n13 with 8 sub-clauses (a-h) bound via cumulative-ack inheritance.
decision.successor_instruction:
Guide-#8 INHERITS this audit-checkpoint methodology as procedural inheritance. RECOMMENDATION: spawn a Stage 4.0.v2 pre-flight audit subagent BEFORE EA-8 begins Stage 4 cutover work. Stage 4 is HIGH risk and continuous-block; mid-stage compound drifts are catastrophic (could force rollback). Pre-flight audit catches drifts in the locked Stage 4 XML against actual disk state; binds amendments under new ack g8-ea8_n1.
Stage 4 has 22+ sub-items; audit scope = all 22. Estimated audit runtime ~60-90 min (more sub-items than Stage 3.X).
LA may decline (Option A — proceed without audit) but the procedural inheritance is now established. See decision d10 below for the audit-checkpoint methodology in detail.

decision.title: Triple-subagent verification methodology at Stage 3.10 close
decision.what:
EA-7's Stage 3 close report was verified by THREE parallel general-purpose subagents spawned by Guide-#7 in one Agent-tool message: - S1 (devplatform-side technical correctness; agentId a25d597add110dd16; ~250 lines) - S2 (BlarAI-side sole-mutation invariant; agentId ac19cfe57b9cad481; ~250 lines) - S3 (procedural completeness + forward-bind preservation; agentId a1582a11e7061f6b6; ~300 lines)
Each subagent had an orthogonal slice. Convergence required across all three for VERIFIED disposition. All three converged on VERIFIED. Each surfaced distinct findings (S1: 5 disk-state findings A1-A5; S2: 3 communication findings F-S2-1..3; S3: 2 cosmetic procedural findings P1-P2). Defense-in-depth audit coverage; zero contradictions across subagents.
decision.successor_instruction:
Guide-#8 INHERITS this triple-subagent verification methodology as procedural inheritance. RECOMMENDATION: at Stage 4 close (when EA-8 reports completion), spawn three parallel verification subagents: - S1: devplatform technical (Vikunja DB migration integrity, MCP config repointing, scheduled task XMLs rewritten, bridge daemon healthy) - S2: BlarAI side state (which files were modified — Stage 4 mutates 3 BlarAI MCP configs unlike Stage 3's STATUS-only) - S3: procedural completeness (all 22 sub-items executed, 8 exit_criteria met, end-to-end smoke test passed)
For Stage 4 the methodology is even more important (HIGH risk; destructive cutover). Triple convergence at close is the architectural backstop against rollback decisions made under information ambiguity.

decision.title: Hardening followups are NON-OPTIONAL (LA directive 2026-04-27)
decision.what:
LA explicitly confirmed at 2026-04-27: hardening followups are non-optional for both DOCUMENTATION cleanup (typos, spec drift, audit-chain narrative repair) AND ARCHITECTURAL cleanup (deferred refactors, post-cutover migration tickets, signature deprecations, naming-coupling tickets). Marking followups as "optional" gives them deferral cover; they end up perpetually deferred, leaving the audit chain with defects that compound over time.
Right framing: "Stage 6.7.5 ticket REQUIRED for cleaning up..." (not "Optional Stage 6 hardening" or "if benefit outweighs cost").
LA may triage PRIORITY at Stage 6 EA execution time; ticket EXISTENCE is non-optional.
decision.successor_instruction:
Guide-#8 SHALL frame all deferred-cleanup items as REQUIRED in verdicts and dispositions. No "optional" or "suggested" framing. The 5 existing Stage 6.7.5 tickets (A-E, see d8 below) are mandatory; any new Stage 6.7.5 items Guide-#8 surfaces during Stage 4 work are also mandatory by default. LA may reduce priority but cannot dismiss ticket creation.
For COMMITTED audit artifacts that already use "suggested"/"optional" framing (rare; Guide-#7's 3.0.v2 verdict scrubbed all such instances), do NOT retroactively amend per no-amend doctrine; instead, the Stage 6 EA's iter-1 gate enumerates ALL accumulated Stage 6.7.5 tickets and treats every one as required regardless of original framing (forward-correction via behavior, not via amending committed artifacts).

decision.title: 5 Stage 6.7.5 hardening tickets bound at Stage 3 close (A through E)
decision.what:
5 Stage 6.7.5 hardening tickets bound during Guide-#7's tenure. All REQUIRED (existence non-optional per d7); LA may triage priority at Stage 6 execution.
**TICKET A — Consolidated locked-artifact amendments (HIGH priority)** Source: g7-ea1_n12 + g7-ea1_n13(c)(i) + EA-7 Anomaly A12 disposition + Guide-#7 g7-ea7_n14(d) expansion. Scope: (1) Stage 3 XML + INFRA_DELTA_v2.md typo + spec drift correction (e.g., escalation-watchdog → escalation_watchdog hyphen-vs-underscore); (2) EA-numbering rewrite `g7-ea1_n*` → `g7-ea7_n*` across the 4 committed verdict files (3.1.v2, 3.2.v2, 3.0.v2, 3.10) for posterity; (3) Stage 3.X audit-checkpoint amendments fold into posterity XML cleanup; (4) cross-reference pointers in STATUS.md Closure Footnotes to audit-evidence MD files (3.0.v2-AUDIT_SUBAGENT_OUTPUT, 3.10-AUDIT_SUBAGENTS_S1_S2_S3, future Stage 4/5/6 evidence files).
**TICKET B — BlarAI README orphan cleanup post-Stage-5 (LOW priority)** Source: g7-ea1_n12.
**TICKET C — devplatform fleet-hygiene refs cleanup (LOW priority)** Source: g7-ea1_n12.
**TICKET D — devplatform parallel-sprints ledger refs (LOW priority)** Source: g7-ea1_n12.
**TICKET E — Stage 3.X audit amendments fold + 3 new sub-items (MED priority)** Source: g7-ea1_n13 + g7-ea7_n14(b) expansion. Scope: (1) original n13(a)-(h) amendments fold into locked Stage 3 XML; (2) NEW sub-item: orphan VS Code task in devplatform/.vscode/tasks.json (delete tools/openvino_contrib_agent task entry); (3) NEW sub-item: copy_manifest_v2.yaml totals divergence — add `notes:` block clarification documenting directory-rollup-vs-git-file-count semantic; (4) NEW sub-item: daily_digest --help text BlarAI staleness — rewrite argparse help to project-agnostic; optional broader scan of platform-tool argparse strings.
decision.successor_instruction: Guide-#8 inherits all 5 tickets. Stage 4 work may surface additional Stage 6.7.5 ticket candidates; Guide-#8 emits ticket F or expands existing tickets per d7 framing rule. Tickets are auto-created via mcp__vikunja__create_task in DevPlatform-Meta project (id=10) at Stage 6.7.5 EA execution time per the existing 6.7.5 ticket-creation pattern from g7-ea1_n12.

decision.title: Stage 4 forward-carry: rr1-rr8 verbatim enumeration (per g7-ea7_n14(c))
decision.what:
Stage 3.10 closure verdict bound n14(c): handoff XML AND Stage 4 EA-8 init pointer MUST enumerate rr1-rr8 + f5_1-f5_4 verbatim (not just by reference to the 3.0.v2 verdict). This ensures Stage 4 EA-8 reads its forward-carry list directly.
**Stage 4 EA-8 residual-risk items (rr1..rr8):** - **rr1 (HIGH)**: Runtime behavior post-MCP-repointing — Stage 4 EA-8 SHALL run an end-to-end MCP smoke test against real Vikunja from devplatform context. Surfaces stop-condition (v) deferred from Stage 3 (n13(d)). - **rr2 (HIGH)**: Cross-file consistency of path rewrites — Stage 4 EA-8 preflight SHALL grep `C:\\Users\\mrbla\\BlarAI\\` across `devplatform/.mcp.json` and `devplatform/.vscode/mcp.json` and assert zero matches. - **rr3 (MEDIUM)**: state.json contamination at devplatform — EA-7 left BlarAI's pause-state snapshot in devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json. Stage 4 EA-8 SHALL decide explicitly: (a) reset to clean baseline, OR (b) carry-forward and natural-resolve. Recommend explicit reset. - **rr4 (LOW)**: __pycache__ pollution — Stage 5 EA SHALL run `Remove-Item -Recurse devplatform/tools/**/__pycache__` before final verification. - **rr5 (MEDIUM)**: Anomaly A3 — `Agents Cadence Monitor` S4U elevation requirement at Stage 4.7. CRITICAL for Stage 4.7 per Stage 0 anomaly recording. - **rr6 (HIGH)**: Vikunja shutdown gate at Stage 4.0/4.1 — fleet-paused state must be re-verified, AND Vikunja process must be stopped (V4=1 → V4=0 transition). - **rr7 (HIGH)**: Worktrees-empty precondition — `git worktree list` on BlarAI must show only main worktree before any destructive Stage 4 operation. - **rr8 (MODERATE, NEW at Stage 3 close)**: devplatform working-tree drift — `M .gitignore` with 7-line tail-block duplication. Stage 4 EA-8 SHALL execute `git checkout -- .gitignore` AS THE FIRST PREFLIGHT ACTION before any Stage 4 work.
**Stage 5 EA forward-items (f5_1..f5_4):** - **f5_1**: F-N5/F-N6/F-N7 path drifts (orphan fleet files, ea_queue, top-level no-ops) — Stage 5 EA fresh-eyes. - **f5_2**: F-N8 BlarAI/.vscode/tasks.json post-Stage-5 cleanup (n13(c)(iii) closes the dependency). - **f5_3 (HIGH)**: F-N10 Stage 5.6 testpaths-removal pattern miss for `tools/gate_stale_cleaner/tests` MUST be included alongside the originally-listed `tools/vikunja_mcp`, `tools/fleet_*`, `tools/autonomy_budget`. - **f5_4**: tools/tests/ removal coordination at Stage 5.1 chokepoint deletion (BlarAI/tools/_vikunja_client.py + BlarAI/tools/_project_context.py deletion requires concomitant deletion of test_vikunja_client_scope.py + test_project_context.py).
decision.successor_instruction: Guide-#8's Stage 4 EA-8 init pointer SHALL enumerate rr1-rr8 verbatim (not just by reference). Same for f5_1-f5_4. Pre-flight Stage 4.0.v2 audit (per d10) may add additional rr items if drift detected; bind under new ack g8-ea8_n1.

decision.title: Stage 4.0.v2 pre-flight audit checkpoint methodology (procedural inheritance)
decision.what:
Per Guide-#7's Stage 3.X audit-checkpoint precedent (decision d5), the methodology is: spawn a fresh general-purpose subagent BEFORE EA-N begins stage execution; subagent inventories all stage sub-items against actual disk state; produce drift inventory; Guide synthesizes amendments under new ack via cumulative-ack inheritance; EA-N receives consolidated verdict + executes with full visibility.
Cost: ~1-2 hours of Guide tenure (subagent spawn + result synthesis + verdict authoring + LA approval cycle). Benefit: catches drift in the locked Stage XML against ~4-day-stale repo state; converts mid-stage STOP cycles (catastrophic during Stage 4 destructive cutover) into pre-stage amendment cycles (low risk).
The Stage 4 spec was authored 2026-04-24; Stage 4 execution begins ~5+ days later. Drift accumulation likely (several Stage 4 sub-items reference paths or files that may have changed during Stages 2-3). Specifically: - Item 4.7 references `C:\Users\mrbla\backups\scheduled_tasks_export\` for 13 task XMLs — verify still populated and complete. - Item 4.7.6 references 5 PS1 scripts under `devplatform/tools/scheduled-tasks/` — verify they all copied correctly per Stage 3 g7-ea1_n11 escalation_watchdog filename correction. - Item 4.7.7 G-3 fix checks for `vikunja-autostart` scheduled task at `\` (root TaskPath) AND `.lnk` shortcut in shell:startup — verify state. - Item 4.8 enables 13 tasks one-by-one — verify all 13 still Disabled and present at `\BlarAI\` TaskPath. - Items 4.4-4.6 reference 3 MCP config paths — verify none have been edited by LA between Stage 3 close and Stage 4 spawn.
Plus the Stage 3.X audit pattern surfaced 1 NEW class of drift per residual-risk warning (each iter finds a new class); Stage 4 likely surfaces 2-3 NEW classes given its broader scope.
decision.successor_instruction:
Guide-#8: STRONGLY RECOMMEND running Stage 4.0.v2 pre-flight audit checkpoint before authoring the Stage 4 EA-8 init pointer. Subagent prompt structure modeled on Guide-#7's 3.0.v2 audit (see `temp_for_responses/3.0.v2-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_7_STAGE3_COMPREHENSIVE_AUDIT_CHECKPOINT.xml` and the audit-evidence MD for prompt structure precedent). Subagent scope: all 22 Stage 4 sub-items vs actual disk state; output drift inventory by item, classified by severity (Class A/B/C/D); cross-item cascade analysis.
LA may decline (proceed without audit) — but the LA's "I will not want to stop midway" constraint argues strongly FOR pre-flight audit since it converts in-stage STOPs (which threaten rollback) into pre-stage amendment cycles.

decision.title: Stage 4 EA session estimate: 1 EA-8 session (continuous block; 4-12 hours real-time)
decision.what:
Stage 4 spec text says: "Must be executed in a single continuous session — do NOT stop in the middle. Between the moment BlarAI copies stop and devplatform copies start, the fleet is offline. Keep the window short."
EA-8 token estimate: ~600-800K tokens for happy path within fresh-session budget (~1M-1.2M). Tight but feasible.
Compounding factors: compound spec drift (~50-100K per drift × 1-3 drifts likely = +50-300K); LA back-and-forth (+30-60K per question). Worst-case ~1M tokens — at the edge of single-session capacity.
EA session count: - **Best case: 1 EA-8 session** — clean run, no compound drifts, ~4-6 hours real-time - **Realistic case: 1 EA-8 session with 1-3 STOP cycles** — Guide-#8 dispositions in-session; ~6-10 hours real-time - **Worst case: 1 EA-8 mid-stage STOP requiring rollback** — Stage 4 rolled back; fresh EA-9 retry in a new session
The continuous-block requirement means EA-8 cannot be replaced mid-stage with EA-9. If EA-8 hits context exhaustion mid-cutover, Stage 4 IS rolled back per the trigger_level_4 rollback procedure. That's the LA's commitment.
decision.successor_instruction:
Guide-#8 should set LA expectations explicitly: 1 EA session (procedural requirement), 4-12 hours real-time commitment, do NOT start without VIKUNJA_TOKEN env var pre-set (Item 4.3 requires it; LA must obtain via Vikunja UI → User settings → API tokens → create with Projects+Tasks read), do NOT start with AI dev surfaces open (Item 4.0 closes them), do NOT start without elevated PowerShell ready for S4U handling (rr5).
Guide-#8 should explicitly recommend pre-flight Stage 4.0.v2 audit checkpoint (d10) to MINIMIZE in-stage STOP cycles. Estimate: pre-flight audit converts ~1-3 STOPs into 0 STOPs (best case) or 0-1 STOPs (realistic case).

open_watchpoints.intro: All watchpoints inherited from Guide-#6 (instance 6→7 brief had 12). Status updates per Guide-#7 instance:
open_watchpoints.watchpoint: Agents Cadence Monitor LogonType=S4U; Stage 4.7 Unregister + Register requires elevated PowerShell. A3 / rr5

G-2 false-green via bare WorkingDirectory rewrite at Stage 4.7. Successor must surface to Stage 4 EA. Stage 4 XML Item 4.7 already has the G-2 fix (regex `(?<=<WorkingDirectory>)C:\\Users\\mrbla\\BlarAI(?=</WorkingDirectory>)`); verify it's correctly applied at Stage 4 close. G-2

Vikunja running on host per g6-ea6_n8 Option B disposition. Stage 4.1 has explicit fail-fast guard (`if ($running) { throw }`). Stage 4 EA-8 SHALL stop Vikunja BEFORE Item 4.1 DB migration; restoration of V4=0 invariant ahead of cutover. g6-ea6_n8 / V4-closure-drift / rr6

F-D1-1 MCP-bus class-method routing migration: when VikunjaClient HTTP delegation lands at Stage 5+ cleanup. Stage 6.7.5-pattern DevPlatform-Meta hardening ticket already in scope. NOT a Stage 4 concern. g6-ea6_n7 / F-D1-1 / D4-DIVERGE-forward
blarai_auxiliary_projects 4 / 7 / 9 investigation at Stage 6 (per Anomaly A10 + Stage 6 hardening directive).

A11 alias-vs-rename optional cleanup (rename --blarai-root → --project-root with deprecation warning). Documented as anomaly per g5-10. Stage 6 hardening ticket if naming hygiene benefit outweighs migration cost. (NOTE: per d7, "optional" framing in this watchpoint is legacy from instance 6 brief; LA-confirmed mature-not-minimal motto upgrades all such items to non-optional. Treat as required.)
VIKUNJA_PASS literal rotation (F-1). Now partially mitigated for devplatform (env-var substitution per n13(c)); full closure post-soak per LA's decision.

watchpoint.recheck_at: before any git stash pop / forever

open_watchpoints.watchpoint: Stale wake_launcher auto-stash at stash@{0} (timestamp sdo 20260424_013002). DO NOT POP through any remaining stage. 13+-cycle byte-identity preservation now; will become 14+ cycle when Stage 4 EA-8 emits its first artifact.
Worktree precondition (`git worktree list` empty) re-verify at Stage 4.0.5 ahead of destructive cutover. rr7
No merge to chore/platform-extraction → main until LA explicitly approves per R10. Merge gate at Stage 5.10 with explicit Lead Architect approval.

watchpoint.recheck_at: every EA report at every cycle

open_watchpoints.watchpoint: EA-6's paraphrase-introduced-error pattern. Now confirmed across multiple instances; EA-7 also exhibited under-enumeration of anomalies in close report (A12 mentioned only; A13+A14 also committed but not flagged in close-report — see F-S2-1). Mature response: independent disk verification on every EA report; never trust paraphrased narratives without checking the disposition files OR comparing close-report claims to actual git diff stats.

Single-commit discipline. Stage 4 EA-8's only valid commits before final commits are docs-only mid-stage handoff XMLs at context-exhaustion threat (per i1-4 inheritance). Stage 4 spec Item 4.12 has TWO commits (one per repo) which is acceptable; mid-stage interim commits are NOT.

Pre-flight Stage 4.0.v2 audit checkpoint (d10) STRONGLY recommended before EA-8 spawn. Stage 4 is destructive + continuous-block; mid-stage compound drifts threaten rollback. Audit-checkpoint methodology converts in-stage STOPs into pre-stage amendment cycles. Cost: ~1-2 hours of Guide-#8 tenure. Benefit: ~1-3 STOP cycles avoided. compound-spec-drift-pattern (Stage 3 had 3 STOPs; Stage 4 likely similar without pre-flight)

Triple-subagent verification at Stage 4 close (d6). Stage 4 destructive cutover is HIGH risk; single-Guide review is insufficient. Three orthogonal subagents (S1 devplatform technical, S2 BlarAI-side state, S3 procedural) at Stage 4.13 close.

devplatform working-tree drift at Stage 3 close (`M .gitignore` 7-line tail duplication). EA-8 SHALL execute `git checkout -- .gitignore` AS THE FIRST PREFLIGHT ACTION on devplatform. rr8 / S1 finding A2

devplatform `tools/autonomy_budget/state.json` carries BlarAI runtime pause-state snapshot. Stage 4 EA-8 SHALL decide explicitly: reset to clean baseline OR carry-forward + natural-resolve. Recommend explicit reset. rr3 / S1 finding A1
EA-numbering convention defect in committed Guide-#7 verdict files (g7-ea1_n10..n13 should be g7-ea7_n*). Per no-amend doctrine; correction folds into Stage 6.7.5 TICKET A scope. A12 / d2 / Ticket A

guide_handoff_brief.anomalies_not_yet_in_status:
STATUS.md is fully updated through 76f6050 (Stage 3 close). A12 + A13 + A14 are recorded; the 6-item Stage 3 Closure Footnotes section captures all dispositions. Guide-#8 inherits a clean STATUS state. The c894ebd commit (Guide-#7 closure verdict) does NOT modify STATUS.md (per Stage 3 invariant; Guide verdicts go to temp_for_responses/, not STATUS).
However, the 5 new findings from Guide-#7's 3.10 closure verdict (A1-A5 from S1, F-S2-1..3 from S2, P1-P2 from S3) are NOT yet captured in STATUS.md — they live only in the closure verdict (3.10-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_7_STAGE3_CLOSE_VERIFIED.xml) and the audit-evidence MD. They are forward-bound via g7-ea7_n14 sub-clauses (a-d) to Stage 4 EA-8 preflight (rr8) + Stage 6.7.5 TICKET E expansion + STATUS-cross-reference enrichment (Ticket A) + Stage 4 init pointer requirement.
Guide-#8 may optionally enrich STATUS.md with these findings during Stage 4 STATUS append at Stage 4.13 close, OR defer to Stage 6.7.5 TICKET A's STATUS-cross-reference enrichment. None requiring immediate STATUS.md mutation. All findings forward-bound via g7-ea7_n14.

la_profile.intro: All 19 traits from instance 6→7 brief carry forward. Re-confirmed during Guide-#7 work. Two new observations as p20 + p21.
la_profile.trait: Non-dev "vibe coder". Re-confirmed throughout Stage 3.
Prefers XML-formatted Guide-to-EA directives via temp_for_responses/.
**"Mature not minimal" motto.** Re-confirmed at Stage 3 close: "thrice over with different subagents if that's possible."
Tables faster than prose.
Forensic analysis discipline; appreciates independent disk verification on EA reports.
Paste-attachment hazards. Not exercised in Guide-#7.
IDE focus reminders are context only.
One numbered list per response block. Maintained throughout.
Welcomes proactive handoff recommendations at boundaries. LA accepted Option B handoff at Stage 3.10 close per stage_boundary_recommendation.
Spell out acronyms on first use.
Guided expertise vs menu-of-options. LA appreciated tabular comparison + Guide recommendation in disposition cycles.
Per-request file-write grants only. Guide writes to temp_for_responses/ are pre-authorized per the GUIDE_PROMPT v2.1 carve-out.

Two-file pattern (XML directive + Markdown brief). Single-file XML pattern was sufficient throughout Guide-#7 dispositions; audit evidence MD pattern introduced (separate evidence file alongside verdict XML).
**"temp_for_responses" folder convention** (lowercase, underscores).
LA verifies XML output meticulously. Sharp catch at 2026-04-27: "This is EA-7. This is NOT EA-1." (d2 EA-numbering correction).
**"Critical operation" + "mature not minimal" = thoroughness signal.** Re-confirmed at Stage 3.10 close: explicit "thrice over with different subagents".

**Strategic context-budget conversations.** LA initiated handoff timing conversation at Stage 3.10 close ("when a clean handoff for the next Guide will likely be"). Same pattern as instance-6→7 transition.

**LA accepts Guide overrides on EA recommendations when justified.** During Guide-#7 era: 3.0.v2 audit-checkpoint approval over EA-7's option-(d) hybrid for Item 3.4; Stage 3.10 closure dispositions over EA-7's "Working tree: clean" claim.

**LA's procedural meta-question discipline at stage transitions.** Re-confirmed at Stage 3.10 close: LA asked "I want to understand what commitment I am making when I start the stage as I will not want to stop midway" before approving Stage 4 spawn. Pattern: LA verifies Guide's understanding of risk + commitment before authorizing.

**LA willing to invest in procedural innovation when risk justifies.** At Stage 3 Item 3.4 STOP, LA accepted Guide-#7's Stage 3.X audit-checkpoint procedural restructuring proposal ("approve Stage 3.X audit checkpoint"). At Stage 3.10 close, LA accepted Guide-#7's triple-subagent verification methodology proposal ("thrice over with different subagents if that's possible"). Both were explicit-investment decisions in mature-not-minimal verification depth.
Pattern: when Guide presents a clear cost/benefit analysis where the cost is bounded (additional 30-90 min Guide tenure) and the benefit is risk reduction (avoided STOP cycles or defense-in-depth audit), LA approves quickly. When the proposal is framed in terms of process maturity ("the surgical-disposition pattern has reached diminishing returns"), LA recognizes and authorizes the structural fix.
Future Guide instances should not hesitate to propose procedural innovations when current approach is breaking down.

**LA wants UPFRONT estimate of commitment before stage spawn for HIGH-risk stages.** At Stage 3.10 close, LA explicitly asked "provide me an estimate on the number of EA sessions likely to be needed for Stage 4. I know it is the HIGH risk cutover, so I want to understand what commitment I am making when I start the stage as I will not want to stop midway."
The LA's "commitment" frame is real-time-hours, not session-count. They need to plan a continuous block of attention. Future Guide instances should provide: (1) Best-case session count (1) (2) Realistic-case session count (1 with 1-3 STOP cycles) (3) Worst-case session count (rollback + retry = 2 effective sessions) (4) Real-time hours estimate (4-12 hours for Stage 4) (5) Pre-prep checklist (e.g., VIKUNJA_TOKEN must be set, AI surfaces closed, elevated PowerShell ready) (6) Risk-mitigation strategy (e.g., pre-flight audit checkpoint to reduce in-stage STOPs)
Future Stage 5 + Stage 6 spawns will likely trigger similar upfront-estimate questions.

active_risk_ids.intro: Risk IDs that proved active during Guide-#7 work. Carryforward + status updates + new.
active_risk_ids.risk: Worktree contamination. Re-verified clean at every Stage 3 cycle.
S4U elevation; CRITICAL for Stage 4.7. See w1 + rr5.
resume_fleet vs unpause_fleet. Function-name discipline held; NEVER state.resume_fleet called across the entire Stages 2+3.
Stage 4.7 false-green via bare WorkingDirectory. Watchpoint w2 — carries to Stage 4 EA-8.
VIKUNJA_PASS literal — devplatform side env-var substituted per n13(c); BlarAI side still plaintext (Stage 4 will repoint via 4.4-4.6 with `${env:VIKUNJA_PASS}` per F-1 fix in Stage 4 spec).
REST vs MCP for Vikunja access.
EA-7 exhibited under-enumeration of anomalies in close report (A12 only mentioned; A13+A14 also committed). See w11 + S2 finding F-S2-1.
V4=1 throughout Guide-#7 era; restoration at Stage 4.1 fail-fast guard. See w3 + rr6.

5 g7-ea1 + 1 g7-ea7 acks all SATISFIED at appropriate Item closures: n10 (Iter 1), n11 (3.1.v2 escalation_watchdog), n12 (3.2.v2 selective copy + 4 hardening tickets), n13 (3.0.v2 audit checkpoint, 8 sub-clauses), n14 (3.10 closure VERIFIED, 4 sub-clauses).

Stage 3 had 3 compound drifts (3.1.v2 + 3.2.v2 + 3.4 STOP); reactive STOP-cycle pattern was non-converging. Resolved via Stage 3.X audit-checkpoint procedural innovation (d5). FORWARD: Stage 4 likely to exhibit similar; pre-flight Stage 4.0.v2 audit recommended (d10 + w13).
EA-1 → EA-7 naming defect across 4 committed Guide-#7 verdict files. Per no-amend; A12 records; Stage 6.7.5 TICKET A absorbs correction. See d2 + w17.
`M .gitignore` 7-line duplication at Stage 3 close. See w15 + rr8.
BlarAI runtime pause-state copied to devplatform via Stage 3.1 recursive copy. See w16 + rr3.
Pre-flight audit pattern. See d5 + d10 + w13.
Defense-in-depth audit at HIGH-risk stage closes. See d6 + w14.

source_doc_revisions.intro: Source-doc revisions during Guide-#7 lifetime. STATUS.md updated; all 7 stage XMLs + MASTER_PLAN + AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW + WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW + RECOVERY + INFRA_DELTA_v2 + ROLLBACK_NOVICE_GUIDE UNCHANGED. 4 Guide-#7 verdict XMLs + 2 audit-evidence MDs added to docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/. 1 EA-7 STATUS append commit. Plus 1 orphaned 3.1.v2 verdict cleanup commit.

revision.commits: 76f6050 (Stage 3 close STATUS append: +59/-5 STATUS.md changes; 6-item Stage 3 Closure Footnotes section added; A12 + A13 + A14 anomalies added; Stage Ledger Stage 3 row PENDING → COMPLETE; Current State table updated for Stage 3 close + Stage 4 transition)
revision.note: STATUS.md is now ~305 lines (was 246 pre-Stage-3-close). All Stage 3 dispositions documented in the 6-item Closure Footnotes set. Append-only discipline preserved; A1-A11 untouched; Recovery Anchors / Vikunja Project IDs / How to Resume sections untouched.

revision.status: 6 Guide-#7 + EA-7 audit chain artifacts added
revision.commits: 77e26a9 (Stage 3 init pointer + EA-7 iter-1 gate + Guide-#7 iter-1 verdict) + 97effe2 (3.2.v2 verdict) + 3118178 (orphaned 3.1.v2 verdict cleanup) + 865c52f (3.0.v2 audit checkpoint verdict + audit subagent evidence MD) + c894ebd (3.10 closure VERIFIED verdict + triple-subagent evidence MD)
revision.files_added_this_instance: 1. 3.0-EA_INITIALIZATION_INSTANCE_1_STAGE3_COPY_TOOLS.xml (Stage 3 EA-7 init pointer with 47 unique-ID predecessor binding acks + 9 g7-ea1 acks n1-n9 + EA-7 iter-1 gate restated bound at 77e26a9) 2. 3.1-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_7_ITERATION_1_APPROVED_STAGE3_EA_1.xml (EA-7 iter-1 gate APPROVED; OQ-1 V0i HEAD drift dispositioned as STATUS-staleness; ack g7-ea1_n10) 3. 3.1.v2-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_7_ITEM_3_1_V2_VERIFIED_SPEC_CORRECTION_AUTHORIZED.xml (escalation_watchdog filename hyphen → underscore option (a) authorized; ack g7-ea1_n11) 4. 3.2.v2-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_7_ITEM_3_2_V2_RECLASSIFICATION_REQUIRED_SELECTIVE_COPY_AUTHORIZED.xml (selective governance 3-of-17 copy + Stage 3.3 forward-bind + Stage 4/5 two-layer audit directive + 4 Stage 6.7.5 hardening tickets; F-N1..F-N11 noted-for-Stage-EA findings; ack g7-ea1_n12) 5. 3.0.v2-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_7_STAGE3_COMPREHENSIVE_AUDIT_CHECKPOINT.xml (Stage 3.X audit-checkpoint procedural innovation; Items 3.4-3.10.v2 amendments; ack g7-ea1_n13 with 8 sub-clauses a-h; F-N11 promotion to Class C; Item 3.4.5 NEW) 6. 3.0.v2-AUDIT_SUBAGENT_OUTPUT_STAGE3_COMPREHENSIVE_DRIFT_INVENTORY.md (audit subagent evidence MD supporting 3.0.v2 verdict) 7. 3.10-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_7_STAGE3_CLOSE_VERIFIED.xml (Stage 3 close VERIFIED via three-subagent convergence; 5 new findings dispositions; ack g7-ea7_n14 sub-clauses a-d; FIRST verdict using corrected `g7-ea7` global naming) 8. 3.10-AUDIT_SUBAGENTS_S1_S2_S3_STAGE3_CLOSE_VERIFICATION_EVIDENCE.md (triple-subagent verification evidence MD supporting 3.10 verdict)
revision.note: 8 audit-chain artifacts total this instance. 4 verdict XMLs + 2 audit-evidence MDs + 1 init pointer + 1 EA gate review. Successor Guide-#8 reads ALL of these as part of Variant B comprehension gate (in addition to all instance-6 artifacts + the 9 Guide-#5 disposition files + 5 EA mid-stage handoffs at ea_outputs/).

Stage 3 substantive work landed in devplatform NOT BlarAI per Stage 3 invariant. devplatform now contains: 7 platform tool dirs (autonomy_budget, fleet_observability, fleet_ops, gate_stale_cleaner, scheduled-tasks, vikunja, vikunja_mcp) + 2 chokepoint helpers (_vikunja_client.py + _project_context.py) + tools/tests/ (7 files) + .mcp.json + .vscode/{mcp.json,tasks.json} + .gitignore + docs/{governance/(4 fleet),runbooks/(13),scheduled/wake_templates/(6),domain8_spike_findings.md,claude_cowork/,claude_projects/(6),CLAUDE_*,CO_LEAD_*,DEC*,DOMAIN*} + .copy_manifest_v2.yaml + pyproject.toml.

source_doc_revisions.revision_no_change: No changes to 7 stage XMLs (01_STAGE0_PREFLIGHT through 07_STAGE6_HARDENING), 00_MASTER_PLAN.md, AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW.md, WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW.md, RECOVERY.md, VERIFICATION_COMMANDS.md, INFRA_DELTA_v2.md, ROLLBACK_NOVICE_GUIDE.md, GUIDE_PROMPT.xml, GUIDE_USAGE.md, CLAUDE.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, devplatform/projects/registry.yaml, BlarAI/.platform/vikunja_project_ids.yaml. Stage 3 was strictly devplatform-side substantive work + BlarAI-side STATUS-only.

first_action_for_successor.step: After your Variant B comprehension gate is complete, briefly acknowledge the handoff and flag any STATUS drift or git advance since 2026-04-28T01:30:00Z. Compare branch_at_handoff_blarai (chore/platform-extraction @ c894ebd), branch_at_handoff_devplatform (main @ 95884a0), status_md_last_commit (76f6050), worktree_state_blarai (clean), worktree_state_devplatform (`M .gitignore` per rr8) against actual repo state. Expected new commits since this handoff: only the LA's commit of GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml itself (this file).

**Read Guide-#7's 8 audit-chain artifacts in addition to the Variant B 11-item mandatory reads:** all files at docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/ that begin with "3.0", "3.1", "3.2", or "3.10" (Guide-#7 era). Plus all instance-6 artifacts (16 Guide-#6 disposition files + 2.6-EA_INITIALIZATION_INSTANCE_5/6 + 2.5-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_V4_QUALITY_REVIEW + 9 Guide-#5 disposition files + 5 EA mid-stage handoffs at ea_outputs/).
Total audit-chain reading: 8 (Guide-#7) + 16 (Guide-#6) + 9 (Guide-#5) + 5 (EA mid-stage handoffs) + 4 (EA comprehension gates iter-1/iter-2) + 4 (initialization pointers EA-3/4/5/6) = ~46 files. Significant reading overhead; the Variant B comprehension gate is robust by design. Plan ~45-75 minutes of disciplined reading.

**DECIDE on Stage 4.0.v2 pre-flight audit checkpoint** (per d5 + d10 + w13). RECOMMENDED. If yes: - Author the audit subagent prompt (model on `temp_for_responses/3.0.v2-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_7_STAGE3_COMPREHENSIVE_AUDIT_CHECKPOINT.xml` and the audit-evidence MD's prompt structure) - Spawn the subagent (general-purpose; thoroughness very thorough) - Subagent scope: all 22 Stage 4 sub-items (4.0, 4.0.5, 4.0.6, 4.1, 4.2, 4.3, 4.4, 4.5, 4.6, 4.6.5, 4.7, 4.7.5, 4.7.6, 4.7.7, 4.8.v2, 4.8 (with 13 task sub-actions), 4.9, 4.10, 4.11, 4.12, 4.13, 4.13.5) vs actual disk state - Output: drift inventory by item, classified by severity (Class A/B/C/D), cross-item cascade analysis - Synthesize into Stage 4.0.v2 verdict; bind amendments under new ack g8-ea8_n1 via cumulative-ack inheritance - Commit verdict + evidence MD If LA declines (Option A — proceed without audit), document the decline in your first_action_for_successor outcome and proceed to step 4 directly.

**Draft the Stage 4 EA-8 initialization pointer XML** following the same pattern as `3.0-EA_INITIALIZATION_INSTANCE_1_STAGE3_COPY_TOOLS.xml`. Adapt for: - EA instance N (EA-8 per global EA-numbering convention; see d2) - Stage 4 entry per docs/platform_separation/05_STAGE4_CUTOVER.xml - Required reading order: 05_STAGE4_CUTOVER.xml + STATUS.md (post-76f6050) + 5 EA mid-stage handoffs + Guide-#7 verdict files (3.0/3.1/3.2/3.0.v2/3.10) + audit-evidence MDs + Guide-#6 dispositions + Guide-#5 dispositions + AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW + WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW + INFRA_DELTA_v2 + RECOVERY + EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2 (V1-V7 source) + 00_MASTER_PLAN + (if Stage 4.0.v2 audit done) Stage 4.0.v2 verdict + audit-evidence MD - Required first actions: (a) `git checkout -- .gitignore` on devplatform per rr8/w15 (FIRST PREFLIGHT ACTION); (b) V0a-V0M pickup verification (HEAD blarai = c894ebd or LA-advance-aware; HEAD devplatform = 95884a0; working tree clean both repos post-rr8 cleanup; pytest tools/ both repos = 351; stash byte-identical 13+-cycle going to 14+-cycle on first edit; state.json invariants; fleet still paused; Vikunja stop-imminent at Item 4.1; no merge to main); (c) iteration-1 comprehension gate emission - Stage 4 work scope per 05_STAGE4_CUTOVER.xml: cutover MCP repointing + Vikunja DB migration + scheduled task XMLs rewrite + 13 tasks re-enabled + bridge daemon from devplatform + end-to-end smoke + STATUS append + fleet resume - Carry-forward acks: 75 acks all SATISFIED at appropriate Item closures (55 v4-era + 4 g6-ea5 + 10 g6-ea6 + 5 g7-ea1 + 1 g7-ea7); new acks g8-ea8_n* per global convention - Forward-active items: w1-w17 watchpoints; rr1-rr8 verbatim enumerated (per n14(c)); f5_1-f5_4 verbatim enumerated; 5 Stage 6.7.5 hardening tickets (A-E) Naming convention: `docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/4.0-EA_INITIALIZATION_INSTANCE_8_STAGE4_CUTOVER.xml`

Provide the LA with: (a) the file path of the init pointer; (b) a short chat-paste message for the new Stage 4 EA-8 chat ("Execute the attached..."); (c) the attachment list (analogous to the Stage 3 attachment list, plus the new Stage 4 init pointer + 05_STAGE4_CUTOVER.xml + 4 Guide-#7 verdict files + 2 audit-evidence MDs + (if produced) Stage 4.0.v2 verdict + evidence + 5 EA mid-stage handoffs + Guide-#6 + Guide-#5 dispositions); (d) the LA pre-prep checklist for Stage 4 spawn (VIKUNJA_TOKEN env var; AI surfaces closed; elevated PowerShell ready; ~4-12 hour real-time block).

Wait for the LA to spawn the Stage 4 EA-8 + paste back the Stage 4 EA-8 iteration-1 comprehension gate. Review per the GUIDE_PROMPT.xml ea_comprehension_gate_review procedure. APPROVED authorizes EA-8 to begin Item 4.0. Then ongoing review of Stage 4 work + STOP dispositions if compound drifts surface (likely 0-3 cycles depending on whether pre-flight audit was done).

**At Stage 4 close** (when EA-8 reports completion at Item 4.13.5), spawn three parallel verification subagents per d6 + w14. Three orthogonal slices: - S1 (devplatform technical): Vikunja DB migration integrity (sha256 match, integrity_check ok), MCP config repointing complete, scheduled task XMLs rewritten + re-registered + last-run-result=0, bridge daemon running from devplatform, end-to-end smoke test result - S2 (BlarAI side state): which BlarAI files were modified (Stage 4 mutates 3 MCP configs unlike Stage 3's STATUS-only); diff scope verification; backup files (.pre_stage4_bak) intact for rollback availability - S3 (procedural completeness): all 22 sub-items executed, 8 exit_criteria met, end-to-end smoke documented, fleet resumed cleanly via state.resume_fleet, Stage 4 STATUS append complete Convergence required across all three for VERIFIED. Synthesize into Stage 4 closure verdict (file: 4.13-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_8_STAGE4_CLOSE_VERIFIED.xml).

prime_stage_4_init_pointer_watchpoints.point: Stage 4 EA-8 init pointer must restate ALL 75 binding acks VERBATIM with status flags. Do NOT summarize or paraphrase. EA-8 must restate them in iteration-1 gate.
Stage 4 EA-8 init pointer must explicitly enumerate rr1-rr8 + f5_1-f5_4 VERBATIM (per n14(c) requirement). NOT just by reference to verdict file.
Stage 4 EA-8 init pointer must include FIRST PREFLIGHT ACTION: `git checkout -- .gitignore` on devplatform (per rr8/w15) BEFORE any other Stage 4 work.

Stage 4 EA-8 init pointer must include EXPLICIT pre-prep checklist for LA: (a) obtain VIKUNJA_TOKEN via Vikunja UI → User settings → API tokens → create with Projects+Tasks read; (b) close all AI dev surfaces (Claude Desktop, VS Code, Claude Code) before spawning EA-8; (c) prepare elevated PowerShell shell ready for S4U handling at Item 4.7 (Agents Cadence Monitor LogonType=S4U); (d) accept ~4-12 hour real-time commitment with continuous block.
Stage 4 EA-8 init pointer must include the V0 pickup verification block. EA-8's iteration-1 gate must report V0 results.

Stage 4 EA-8 init pointer must explicitly forward the EA-paraphrase-error pattern as a discipline reminder (per w11). EA-7 also exhibited under-enumeration of anomalies in close report. Mature response is independent disk verification + close-report cross-check against actual git diff stats.

Stage 4 EA-8 init pointer should reference the 6-item Stage 3 Closure Footnotes in STATUS.md as the canonical source for Stage 3 disposition narratives (NOT EA-7's chat-narrative paraphrases per w11).

Stage 4 EA-8 init pointer must include explicit STOP-and-report instructions for: (a) stop-condition (v) deferred from Stage 3 (n13(d)) — VIKUNJA_PASS env-var resolution test against real MCP consumer; (b) any state.json mutation attempts (rr3/w16); (c) any worktree-list-non-empty discovery (rr7/w9).

Stage 4 EA-8 init pointer must include the audit-checkpoint methodology and triple-subagent verification methodology as procedural inheritance — EA-8 should expect Guide-#8 to spawn subagents at appropriate points; EA-8 cooperates without confusion.

do_not_list.intro: Carry forward all 24 prohibitions from instance 6→7 brief (dn1-dn24). Add 6 new (dn25-dn30) for instance 7 work products + Stage 4 specifics.
do_not_list.prohibition: Do NOT re-chase STATUS.md line 11 v2-banner HEAD staleness. Known chicken-and-egg.
Pre-execution invariant past-tense issue resolved at Stage 1 EA close.
Do NOT edit, or allow any Stage 3-5 EA to edit, BlarAI's CLAUDE.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, or AGENTS.md. V8 / 6.1.v2 deferred.
Do NOT confuse fleet pause with Vikunja server stop. Different concerns.
Do NOT relax acknowledgment-wait for Stage 4 EA-8. Strict verbatim enforcement of 75-ack chain.
Do NOT debug a "physform-extraction" typo.
Do NOT regenerate or revise Stage 0 / Stage 1 / Stage 2 / Stage 3 baseline JSON / exit reports / mid-stage handoffs / closure verdicts. Frozen audit artifacts.
Do NOT emit a handoff after only a partial Stage UNLESS LA-requested OR per i1-4 trigger.
Do NOT modify this handoff brief or any predecessor handoff brief in git history. Permanent records.
Do NOT allow any Stage 4-5 EA to run `git stash pop` / `git stash apply` on stash@{0}.
Do NOT delete, modify, or "improve" GUIDE_POST_STAGE1_CLEANUP_DIRECTIVE.xml or GUIDE_POST_STAGE1_CLEANUP_BRIEF.md. Audit artifacts.
Do NOT assume LA's file-write permission carries forward across instances.
Do NOT delete or "clean up" the Guide disposition files in temp_for_responses/.
Do NOT trust the EA's "I did X" reports without disk-level verification. EA-7 exhibited close-report under-enumeration (A12 only, A13+A14 also committed). Future EAs likely similar.
Do NOT accept a partial-commit Stage-N mid-stage handoff. Mid-stage handoff XMLs are docs-only.
Do NOT silently approve scope expansion. Default disposition for any audit-surfaced PS1 is no-action with Appendix A.1 citation.
Do NOT shortcut analysis to save context window. "Mature not minimal." Depth wins over speed.
Do NOT confuse the legacy fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py with the new chokepoint tools/_vikunja_client.py.
Do NOT defer in-scope defect closures to a different stage when the fix is trivial.
Do NOT skip the "mature not minimal" disk-verification step on EA reports that look clean.
Do NOT spawn Stage 4 EA-8 without first authoring the Stage 4 EA-8 initialization pointer.
Do NOT skip the Variant B comprehension gate for Guide-#8 (or any successor).
Do NOT modify the GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml file once it's committed. Audit anchor.
Do NOT skip independent disk verification on Stage 4 EA-8 narrative summaries.

**Do NOT silently correct the EA-numbering defect in committed Guide-#7 verdict files.** Per d2 + Anomaly A12: the `g7-ea1_n10..n13` labels in 4 committed verdict files are documentation defects; per no-amend doctrine for committed audit artifacts, these are NOT retroactively rewritten. Stage 4 EA-8 + Stage 5 EA + Stage 6 EAs all restate VERBATIM as labeled. Correction folds into Stage 6.7.5 TICKET A.

**Do NOT mark Stage 6.7.5 hardening tickets as "optional" or "suggested".** Per d7 + LA directive 2026-04-27: hardening followups are NON-OPTIONAL for both documentation AND architectural cleanup. Right framing: "Stage 6.7.5 ticket REQUIRED for cleaning up..." LA may triage priority; existence is non-optional.

**Do NOT skip the pre-flight Stage 4.0.v2 audit checkpoint without explicit LA decline.** Per d5 + d10 + w13: Stage 4 is HIGH risk + continuous-block; mid-stage compound drifts threaten rollback. The pre-flight audit converts in-stage STOPs into pre-stage amendments. If LA declines (Option A — proceed without audit), document the decline; otherwise default to running the audit.

**Do NOT skip triple-subagent verification at Stage 4 close.** Per d6 + w14: Stage 4 destructive cutover is HIGH risk; single-Guide review is insufficient. Three orthogonal subagents (S1 devplatform technical, S2 BlarAI side state, S3 procedural) at Stage 4.13 close. Convergence required for VERIFIED.

**Do NOT spawn Stage 4 EA-8 without LA pre-prep verification.** Per w13/p21/p20: Stage 4 requires LA to (a) obtain VIKUNJA_TOKEN env var pre-set; (b) close all AI dev surfaces; (c) prepare elevated PowerShell ready; (d) accept 4-12 hour real-time commitment block. Spawn before pre-prep risks Item 4.3 STOP (VIKUNJA_TOKEN missing) or Item 4.0 partial close (surfaces still open).

**Do NOT propagate the EA-1 vs EA-7 naming defect into NEW Guide-#8 verdicts.** Per d2: Guide-#8 emits new acks under `g8-ea8_n*` (NOT `g8-ea1_n*`). Cumulative-ack chain restatement uses verbatim labels for committed prior verdicts (g7-ea1_n10..n13) + corrected naming for new verdicts (g7-ea7_n14 for prior, g8-ea8_n* for Guide-#8 new).
